domains.rationale: Official Google developer documentation and support hub for Google Maps Platform, including product docs, getting started guides, API references, and customer care pages.
Official Google Maps Platform marketing and product site, including product overview, blog/news, support resources, and API landing pages.
Official Google Cloud domain hosting Google Maps Platform terms, technical support guidelines, and status/support hub content relevant to product behavior and service operations.

elements.label: A first remote terminal receives an address and event data and uses a database to correlate the address to location-based data.
A second terminal includes a map display and receives location-based data from a location system based on the second terminal's own location.
search_queries: Maps SDK for Android my location map view
Maps SDK for iOS user location map view
Maps JavaScript API map display marker
location bias nearby search Places API

elements.label: The second terminal transmits its location-based data to the first terminal over a communication channel.
search_queries: Maps SDK for Android marker location

elements.label: The first terminal stores the second terminal's location-based data in memory.
When the stored location-based data and event data satisfy specified criteria, the first terminal sends the correlated location-based data and event data to the second terminal.
search_queries: Places API nearby search location bias
Maps JavaScript API route origin destination

elements.label: The second terminal automatically inputs the received location-based data into the display unit to show a map that includes the first address.
search_queries: Maps SDK for Android camera map view
Maps SDK for iOS annotation user location

urls.title: Geocoding API v4 overview
urls.snippet: The Geocoding API is a service that accepts a place as an address, latitude and longitude coordinates, or Place ID. It converts the address into latitude ...
urls.rationale: Official Geocoding API overview states it accepts an address or Place ID and converts the address into latitude/longitude coordinates, which directly supports address-to-location correlation and map placement.

urls.title: Geocode an address | Geocoding API
urls.snippet: Geocoding translates an address into a location on a map. When you geocode an address, the response contains the: Place ID of the location; Latitude and ...
urls.rationale: Docs explicitly say geocoding translates an address into a location on a map and return latitude/longitude, directly evidencing address lookup and map display behavior.

urls.title: Get Started with the Geocoding API v4
urls.snippet: Geocoding is the process of converting addresses (like a street address) into geographic coordinates (like latitude and longitude), which you can use to place ...
urls.rationale: Get started page for Geocoding API v4 says it converts addresses into geographic coordinates used to place markers on a map, matching the claim's address-to-location and map display limitations.

urls.title: Place Autocomplete Address Form | Maps JavaScript API
urls.snippet: The Place Autocomplete Address Form sample captures selected address components from the Google Places database, and uses them to populate an address form.
urls.rationale: Official sample shows Place Autocomplete capturing address components from Google Places and populating an address form, which is strong evidence for address lookup/correlation and automatic input of received location data into a display form/map workflow.

urls.title: Location Data | Maps SDK for Android
urls.snippet: The My Location layer provides a simple way to display a device's location on the map. ... My Location button to show your user their current position on the map.
urls.rationale: Official Android SDK docs describe the My Location layer showing the device's current position on a map, which aligns with a terminal receiving location-based data based on its own location and using it in map display behavior.

urls.title: Show My Location | Maps SDK for Android
urls.snippet: This example shows how to enable the My Location layer and demonstrates the My Location button to show the device's current position.
urls.rationale: Sample documentation for enabling the My Location layer and button to display the device's current position on the map provides strong evidence for a second terminal using its own location with a map display.

urls.title: Select Current Place and Show Details on a Map
urls.snippet: This tutorial demonstrates building an Android app to find the device's location and display nearby places using the Maps SDK, Places SDK, and Fused Location ...
urls.rationale: Tutorial shows an Android app retrieving the device's location and displaying nearby places on a map, supporting location-based input into a map display unit and automatic map presentation.

urls.title: Geocoding FAQ | Geocoding API
urls.snippet: Geocoding converts addresses into geographic coordinates to be placed on a map. Reverse Geocoding finds an address based on geographic coordinates or place ...
urls.rationale: Official Geocoding FAQ directly states that geocoding converts addresses into geographic coordinates to be placed on a map, matching address-to-location-based-data correlation.

urls.title: Geocoding Service | Maps JavaScript API
urls.snippet: This example demonstrates geocoding and reverse geocoding on a map using the Google Maps JavaScript API. · Users can enter an address in a text field to geocode ...
urls.rationale: Example explicitly demonstrates geocoding and reverse geocoding with users entering an address to geocode on a map, which is strong evidence for address-to-location lookup.

urls.title: Place Autocomplete and Directions | Maps JavaScript API
urls.snippet: This sample demonstrates using the Google Maps JavaScript API to calculate and display routes between two locations selected by the user using autocomplete.
urls.rationale: Maps JavaScript sample combines place autocomplete with directions and displays routes between user-selected locations, which is the closest evidence here for transmitting/using location inputs and automatically showing a map route.

urls.title: Place Details (New) | Places API
urls.snippet: The Place Details API returns comprehensive information about a place, such as its address, phone number, ratings, and reviews, using a unique place ID.
urls.rationale: Official Places API docs state Place Details returns comprehensive information about a place using a unique place ID, supporting address/place lookup and storage of place/location data, though it does not describe dispatch criteria or terminal-to-terminal transmission.

urls.title: Method: places.searchText | Places API
urls.snippet: LocationBias. The region to search. This location serves as a bias which means results around given location might be returned.
urls.rationale: Official Places API reference for text search with location bias; supports location-based querying and conditional result selection, aligning with map/location data transmission and criteria-based dispatch behavior.
